[
    {
        "Answers": {
            "A": "Autolaunched Flow called as a Subflow",
            "B": "Copy the elements into every flow",
            "C": "Separate validation rules for each object",
            "D": "Screen Flow with hidden fields"
        },
        "CorrectAnswers": [
            "A"
        ],
        "Reasoning": "Reusable logic belongs in an autolaunched flow invoked by parent flows as a subflow.",
        "category": "Automation",
        "Status": "APPROVED",
        "PK": "EXAM#ADMIN",
        "Category": "Automation",
        "QuestionText": "The leadership team at Evergreen Equipment asks Owen, the sales operations analyst, to adjust Salesforce. The team prefers a native Salesforce feature rather than custom code. Multiple flows need the same tax calculation logic and the logic should be updated in one place. Which architecture is best?",
        "SK": "QUESTION#Q_409"
    },
    {
        "Answers": {
            "A": "After-save flow because all record-triggered flows require committed records",
            "B": "Screen flow launched from a quick action",
            "C": "Scheduled flow running every hour",
            "D": "Before-save flow for fast field updates"
        },
        "CorrectAnswers": [
            "D"
        ],
        "Reasoning": "Before-save flows are optimized for updating fields on the triggering record before it is committed. After-save flows are for actions and related records, scheduled flows run independently of the save event, and screen flows require user interaction.",
        "category": "Automation",
        "Status": "APPROVED",
        "PK": "EXAM#ADMIN",
        "Category": "Automation",
        "QuestionText": "A record-triggered flow at Beacon Security Commercial Division updates the Account Rating based on Annual Revenue whenever an Account is saved. The flow only changes fields on the triggering Account and should run as efficiently as possible. Which flow path should be used?",
        "SK": "QUESTION#Q_809"
    },
    {
        "Answers": {
            "A": "After-save path for actions and related records",
            "B": "Validation rule with an email template",
            "C": "Before-save path for fast field updates",
            "D": "Formula field on Account"
        },
        "CorrectAnswers": [
            "A"
        ],
        "Reasoning": "Creating related records and sending emails require an after-save flow because the triggering record must already be saved and available for actions. Before-save flows are for changing fields on the triggering record only.",
        "category": "Automation",
        "Status": "APPROVED",
        "PK": "EXAM#ADMIN",
        "Category": "Automation",
        "QuestionText": "A flow at MetroLink Utilities Operations Division must create a related Audit Log record and send an email whenever a high-risk Account is created. The Account ID must already exist before the related record is created. Which path should the administrator use?",
        "SK": "QUESTION#Q_822"
    },
    {
        "Answers": {
            "A": "Before-save path for fast field updates",
            "B": "Formula field on Account",
            "C": "Validation rule with an email template",
            "D": "After-save path for actions and related records"
        },
        "CorrectAnswers": [
            "D"
        ],
        "Reasoning": "Creating related records and sending emails require an after-save flow because the triggering record must already be saved and available for actions. Before-save flows are for changing fields on the triggering record only.",
        "category": "Automation",
        "Status": "APPROVED",
        "PK": "EXAM#ADMIN",
        "Category": "Automation",
        "QuestionText": "A flow at Falcon Outdoor Transformation Division must create a related Audit Log record and send an email whenever a high-risk Account is created. The Account ID must already exist before the related record is created. Which path should the administrator use?",
        "SK": "QUESTION#Q_846"
    },
    {
        "Answers": {
            "A": "REGEX()",
            "B": "PRIORVALUE()",
            "C": "VLOOKUP()",
            "D": "ISNEW()",
            "E": "ISCHANGED()"
        },
        "CorrectAnswers": [
            "D"
        ],
        "Reasoning": "ISNEW() identifies records being created. ISCHANGED and PRIORVALUE are primarily about edits/previous values.",
        "Status": "APPROVED",
        "PK": "EXAM#ADMIN",
        "Category": "Automation",
        "QuestionText": "After a process change at Bluewave Insurance, Ben needs to choose the most maintainable Salesforce configuration. The business wants the most standard declarative solution. A validation rule should require Reason only when the record is first created. Which function is essential?",
        "category": "Automation",
        "SK": "QUESTION#Q_148"
    },
    {
        "Answers": {
            "A": "VLOOKUP()",
            "B": "REGEX()",
            "C": "BEGINS()",
            "D": "INCLUDES()",
            "E": "CONTAINS()"
        },
        "CorrectAnswers": [
            "B"
        ],
        "Reasoning": "REGEX validates a text value against a specific pattern.",
        "Status": "APPROVED",
        "PK": "EXAM#ADMIN",
        "Category": "Automation",
        "QuestionText": "A department at Crescent Financial reports a problem to Keira, the platform administrator. The requirement must respect normal Salesforce security behavior. A tracking ID must match two uppercase letters, a hyphen, and six digits. Which validation function is best?",
        "category": "Automation",
        "SK": "QUESTION#Q_149"
    },
    {
        "Answers": {
            "A": "Dashboard filter",
            "B": "App Manager Utility Item",
            "C": "Add Error element/custom error in the record-triggered flow",
            "D": "Campaign Member Status",
            "E": "Report bucket"
        },
        "CorrectAnswers": [
            "C"
        ],
        "Reasoning": "Modern record-triggered flows can surface custom errors to prevent a record save when criteria fail.",
        "category": "Automation",
        "Status": "APPROVED",
        "PK": "EXAM#ADMIN",
        "Category": "Automation",
        "QuestionText": "A department at Meridian Partners reports a problem to George, the system administrator. The team prefers a native Salesforce feature rather than custom code. An administrator wants to prevent a save with a custom error directly from a record-triggered flow when criteria are not met. Which Flow capability can be used?",
        "SK": "QUESTION#Q_303"
    },
    {
        "Answers": {
            "A": "Screen Flow",
            "B": "Approval Process",
            "C": "Record-triggered flow that runs before delete",
            "D": "Scheduled Flow",
            "E": "After-delete record-triggered flow"
        },
        "CorrectAnswers": [
            "C"
        ],
        "Reasoning": "Before-delete flows can access the record data and block deletion via errors/fault handling. There is no after-delete record-triggered flow option.",
        "category": "Automation",
        "Status": "APPROVED",
        "PK": "EXAM#ADMIN",
        "Category": "Automation",
        "QuestionText": "After a process change at Crescent Financial, Jonah needs to choose the most maintainable Salesforce configuration. The solution should minimize long-term maintenance. A record must be archived before it is deleted, and deletion should stop if archive creation fails. Which flow trigger should be used?",
        "SK": "QUESTION#Q_475"
    },
    {
        "Answers": {
            "A": "Validation rule with an email template",
            "B": "After-save path for actions and related records",
            "C": "Before-save path for fast field updates",
            "D": "Formula field on Account"
        },
        "CorrectAnswers": [
            "B"
        ],
        "Reasoning": "Creating related records and sending emails require an after-save flow because the triggering record must already be saved and available for actions. Before-save flows are for changing fields on the triggering record only.",
        "category": "Automation",
        "Status": "APPROVED",
        "PK": "EXAM#ADMIN",
        "Category": "Automation",
        "QuestionText": "A flow at BrightPath Consulting Partner Division must create a related Audit Log record and send an email whenever a high-risk Account is created. The Account ID must already exist before the related record is created. Which path should the administrator use?",
        "SK": "QUESTION#Q_858"
    },
    {
        "Answers": {
            "A": "Record-triggered flow optimized for Actions and Related Records",
            "B": "Scheduled Flow",
            "C": "Screen Flow",
            "D": "Process Builder",
            "E": "Record-triggered flow optimized for Fast Field Updates"
        },
        "CorrectAnswers": [
            "E"
        ],
        "Reasoning": "Before-save fast field updates are the most performant option for updating fields on the triggering record.",
        "Status": "APPROVED",
        "PK": "EXAM#ADMIN",
        "Category": "Automation",
        "QuestionText": "After a process change at Velocity Tech Solutions, Ravi needs to choose the most maintainable Salesforce configuration. The solution should minimize long-term maintenance. A field on the triggering Account should be set automatically before the record is saved for performance. Which flow optimization should be used?",
        "category": "Automation",
        "SK": "QUESTION#Q_268"
    },
    {
        "Answers": {
            "A": "Get each related record individually inside the loop",
            "B": "Perform one Update Records outside the loop using the collection",
            "C": "Run Update Records inside the loop",
            "D": "System context bypasses governor limits",
            "E": "Use assignments inside the loop to build a collection"
        },
        "CorrectAnswers": [
            "B",
            "E"
        ],
        "Reasoning": "Bulkified flows avoid DML and Get Records inside loops, collecting changes and updating once.",
        "category": "Automation",
        "Status": "APPROVED",
        "PK": "EXAM#ADMIN",
        "Category": "Automation",
        "QuestionText": "The leadership team at Quantum Foods asks Maya, the platform administrator, to adjust Salesforce. The solution should minimize long-term maintenance. A flow updates many child records. Which two design practices avoid governor-limit issues? (Select Two)",
        "SK": "QUESTION#Q_654"
    },
    {
        "Answers": {
            "A": "Profile",
            "B": "Permission Set Group",
            "C": "Queue",
            "D": "Related User",
            "E": "Public Group"
        },
        "CorrectAnswers": [
            "C",
            "D",
            "E"
        ],
        "Reasoning": "Approval steps can assign to users, related users, queues, roles, or public groups depending setup; profiles and permission set groups are permission constructs, not approvers.",
        "category": "Automation",
        "Status": "APPROVED",
        "PK": "EXAM#ADMIN",
        "Category": "Automation",
        "QuestionText": "While testing a new rollout at Universal Containers, Hannah discovers a configuration gap. The change should not introduce unnecessary custom objects or Apex. Which three approver target types can be assigned directly in approval steps? (Select Three)",
        "SK": "QUESTION#Q_646"
    },
    {
        "Answers": {
            "A": "Entry Criteria",
            "B": "Record Editability",
            "C": "Default Workflow User",
            "D": "Validation Rule on save",
            "E": "Initial Submission Action"
        },
        "CorrectAnswers": [
            "A"
        ],
        "Reasoning": "Approval process entry criteria determine whether a submitted record is allowed into the process.",
        "category": "Automation",
        "Status": "APPROVED",
        "PK": "EXAM#ADMIN",
        "Category": "Automation",
        "QuestionText": "While testing a new rollout at Universal Containers, Wendy discovers a configuration gap. The change should not introduce unnecessary custom objects or Apex. A record should enter an approval process only if Priority is High and Type is Problem. Which part of the approval process enforces this?",
        "SK": "QUESTION#Q_408"
    },
    {
        "Answers": {
            "A": "Field Update",
            "B": "Call Apex directly",
            "C": "Screen Flow",
            "D": "Email Alert",
            "E": "Create Records"
        },
        "CorrectAnswers": [
            "A",
            "D"
        ],
        "Reasoning": "Approval processes support standard actions such as Field Updates and Email Alerts. Creating records, directly calling Apex, or launching screen flows are not standard approval action types in the approval action menu.",
        "category": "Automation",
        "Status": "APPROVED",
        "PK": "EXAM#ADMIN",
        "Category": "Automation",
        "QuestionText": "When an expense report is finally rejected at MetroLink Utilities Regional Division, the status should update to Closed and the submitter should receive a standard email. Which two standard approval actions meet this requirement? (Select Two)",
        "SK": "QUESTION#Q_837"
    },
    {
        "Answers": {
            "A": "Approval entry criteria",
            "B": "Before-save fast field update",
            "C": "Validation Rule",
            "D": "Login Flow",
            "E": "After-save record-triggered flow / Actions and Related Records"
        },
        "CorrectAnswers": [
            "E"
        ],
        "Reasoning": "Creating related records and sending emails require after-save actions because the parent record ID/save context is needed.",
        "category": "Automation",
        "Status": "APPROVED",
        "PK": "EXAM#ADMIN",
        "Category": "Automation",
        "QuestionText": "A department at Silverline Travel reports a problem to Hannah, the platform administrator. The team prefers a native Salesforce feature rather than custom code. A flow must create a related child record and send an email after an Account is saved. Which flow path is appropriate?",
        "SK": "QUESTION#Q_473"
    },
    {
        "Answers": {
            "A": "PRIORVALUE()",
            "B": "IMAGE()",
            "C": "HYPERLINK()",
            "D": "VLOOKUP()"
        },
        "CorrectAnswers": [
            "A"
        ],
        "Reasoning": "PRIORVALUE() compares a field's previous value to its new value in validation logic, making it useful for detecting prohibited transitions. VLOOKUP checks values in a custom object, while IMAGE and HYPERLINK are display functions.",
        "category": "Automation",
        "Status": "APPROVED",
        "PK": "EXAM#ADMIN",
        "Category": "Automation",
        "QuestionText": "The operations team at Redwood Finance Transformation Division wants a validation rule to prevent users from changing a closed Opportunity back to an open stage after it has been marked Closed Won or Closed Lost. Which formula function is most useful for detecting that the prior stage was closed?",
        "SK": "QUESTION#Q_841"
    },

        "Answers": {

            "B": "Final Rejection Actions only",
            "C": "A validation rule on approval history",
            "D": "Recall action",
            "E": "Step rejection behavior: Go back to the previous step"
        },
        "CorrectAnswers": [
            "E"
        ],
        "Reasoning": "Approval steps can be configured so rejection goes back to the previous step instead of performing all rejection actions.",
        "category": "Automation",
        "Status": "APPROVED",
